message:
feat(abi): delegate getopt short-scanner to core::getopt::step_short (bd-go-3, closes bd-go-epic)

Replaces parse_getopt_short's ~140-line pointer-arithmetic scanner in
unistd_abi.rs with a thin marshalling shim over the pure-safe Rust
state machine added in bd-go-2. Removes local GetoptArgMode /
getopt_arg_mode / getopt_prefers_colon (now imported from
frankenlibc_core::getopt).

The shim:
  1. Materializes argv as Vec<&[u8]> via CStr (rejects NULL slots).
  2. Reconstructs GetoptState from libc_optind / libc_optopt and a
     persistent static GETOPT_NEXTCHAR: Option<ArgRef>.
  3. Calls core::getopt::step_short.
  4. Marshals state.optarg back to a *mut c_char by computing
     argv[idx] + offset, syncs optind/optopt to globals, and stashes
     the residual nextchar.

parse_getopt_long is unchanged in shape — it still owns long-option
matching — but now calls core::getopt_prefers_colon and resets
GETOPT_NEXTCHAR to Option<ArgRef>::None instead of a raw null pointer.

Verification:
  - tests/conformance_diff_getopt.rs: 3/3 pass (basic short opts,
    long opts, coverage report)
  - frankenlibc-abi lib: 173/173 pass

Closes bd-go-epic. Net win: short-option scanner is now layout-
independent, reachable from #![deny(unsafe_code)] core, and unit-
tested in isolation (25 core::getopt tests).
